result:
The spec is fully implemented. I added the three new operations to `FolderSync.cs`, leaving all existing operations untouched:

- **`RecordDelete`** (FolderSync.cs:170) — leaves the tombstone unchanged when its existing stamp is `>= stamp` (an absent entry reads as `-1`, so it's always written then), otherwise upserts via the existing `WriteIndexEntry`, inheriting its replace-in-place/append rules.
- **`SyncTwoWay`** (FolderSync.cs:213) — snapshots both index files at the start, processes source names in file order, then target-only names (relative to the starting source snapshot) in target order. A shared `SyncOneName` helper applies the policy per name: tombstone stamp `>= max(s, t)` removes the entry from each index where present (one action per removal), otherwise the newer side is upserted into the other (one action), and equal stamps do nothing. The tombstone file is only ever read.
- **`SyncWithPolicy`** (FolderSync.cs:242) — delegates to `SyncTwoWay` when `twoWay` is true, otherwise to the existing `Sync`, which never touches the tombstone.

The project builds with 0 warnings/0 errors, and I verified behavior with a throwaway console project (since deleted) exercising the new functions against real files: newer-wins copies in both directions, tombstoned deletion removing from both sides with two actions counted, lower-stamp `RecordDelete` calls ignored, tombstone file left unmodified by `SyncTwoWay`, and the one-way policy copying/pruning entries even when tombstones exist for them. All results matched the spec. Used 2 of the 10 build/test cycles.
